specific_location: West 5th Ave about 1/4 mile west of Nucla
description: Average size flycatcher, grayish back, off white throat and chest with light gray streaking, belly and undertailed coverts salmon colored. The head was slightly peeked. The eye was dark with a gray patch behind the eye which matched the color of the back. The tail was darker than the back and in flight showed some white in the outer tail feathers. The bill was small in diameter but about the lenth of other medium size flycatchers. Wing bars were only slightly noticable.Behaviors: Flycatcher was sitting on a fence attempting to find food. After watching the bird for a minute or two at very close range I back up the car to increase my distance from the bird. The bird flew a good distance to the top of a elm sapling. I watched to bird in the scope for a few more minutes but the flycatcher was still when I drove back home. I did not see the bird catch any food.Call: none heardPlumage: adult female
